question: How do `chunks_fts` rows get kept in sync with `chunks`?
ground_truth: Three SQLite triggers on the base `chunks` table (`internal/store/store.go:159-176`): INSERT mirrors content/path/kind into `chunks_fts`; UPDATE issues a `chunks_fts` delete+insert; DELETE removes the row. There is also a one-time backfill (`chunks_fts(rebuild)`) for databases that pre-date the FTS5 migration (~line 254-261).

claude.result:
Three SQLite triggers on the `chunks` table, created in `migrate` (`internal/store/store.go:159-176`):

| Trigger | Event | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `chunks_ai` | `AFTER INSERT` | `INSERT INTO chunks_fts(rowid, content, path, kind)` |
| `chunks_ad` | `AFTER DELETE` | FTS5 tombstone: `INSERT INTO chunks_fts(chunks_fts, rowid, ...) VALUES('delete', ...)` |
| `chunks_au` | `AFTER UPDATE` | Delete + reinsert in `chunks_fts` |

`chunks_fts` is an FTS5 **external-content** table (`content='chunks', content_rowid='id'`), so it stores only tokenizer state — no duplicate text on disk. The triggers are the only sync mechanism; Go code never writes to `chunks_fts` directly.

There is also a one-time backfill for pre-FTS5 databases: `INSERT INTO chunks_fts(chunks_fts) VALUES('rebuild')` runs during migration (~`store.go:254-261`) when upgrading an older index.
